notes: Set2 Q3: source answer was blank; filled as 'sender' (C).
Set2 'a ___ indicates the action done by the subject': corrected to Verb (A); source key had Adverb.
Set3 Q82 (plural forms) was open-ended fill-in, not multiple choice - omitted from quiz pool.

Effective communication in education promotes:
Communication is a non-stop ______.
Communication is a part of ________ skills.
The ______ is the person who transmits the message.
______ is the person who notices and decodes and attaches some meaning to a message.
Message is any signal that triggers the response of a ______.
The response to a sender message is called ______.
______ context refers to the relationship between the sender and the receiver.
______ context refers to the similarity of backgrounds between the sender and the receiver.
______ refers to all those factors that disrupt the communication.
Environmental barriers are the same as ______ noise.
Our dress code is an example of ______ communication.
Communication strengthens ______ & ______ relationship in an organization.
______ communication includes tone of voice, body language, facial expressions etc.
When there is similarity of background between the sender and the receiver such as age, language, nationality, religion, gender, then this is called ______ context.
Letter, e-mail, telephone are examples of ______.
Understanding ______ different parts of speech forms the base of learning grammar.
It is of paramount importance that one needs to construct a ______ sentence in the day to day affairs.
A ______ may be defined as the name of a person, place or thing.
According to Hoban, communication is the ______ interchange of thought or idea.
The person who transmits the message is called the ______.
Proper nouns always begin with ______ letters.
______ nouns require capitalization only if they start the sentence or are part of a title.
Once the message is encoded in a desired format, it is transferred through a medium called ______.
The nouns which cannot be felt, seen or heard are called ______.
The information which is transferred to the receiver has to be interpreted; this process is called ______.
All communication events have a ______.
Personifications of strength and violence are considered as ______ gender.
The message may be misinterpreted because of ______.
The environment in which the transmitter or receiver are should be ______.
A noun that denotes neither a male nor a female is ______ gender.
Countries when referred to by names are also considered ______.
The Christian sign of the ______ is a gesture pertaining to religion and spirituality.
In oral communication there is a possibility of immediate ______.
In oral communication the speaker can observe the listener's ______ to what is being related.
Nouns that end in Y but have a consonant before Y form their plural by dropping Y and adding ______.
While talking to friends you do not pay attention to the skills of ______ communication.
In oral presentation outside your organization you must first give the audience a ______ of your organization.
A and An are the ______ articles.
The ______ are used to present using overhead projectors.
Any word that adds more meaning to the noun is called an ______.
A ______ indicates the action done by the subject.
A ______ is a word which connects words, phrases, clauses or sentences.
During presentation using an OHP, one can read information line by line using an opaque sheet to cover the transparency to minimize distraction. This technique is called ______.
Another thing you have to avoid is adding to OHPs with a ______ during a talk.
It is important to consider proper ______ in the room where you are giving your presentation.
______ listening means learning through conversation.
In ______ listening the difference between the sounds is identified.
The ______ is an exclamation mark.
Evaluative listening is also called ______.
The ______ is the action or description that occurs in the sentence.
The ______ speech is also called reported speech.
The writing process includes beginning, middle and end.
______ is a well organized piece of prose on a given topic.
______ process answers the questions about who, where, why, what and how concerning a given topic.
Development of ideas can be categorized into the ______ stage of writing.
______ involves writing down all the ideas gotten during brainstorming.
One of these is NOT a method of developing ideas.
The ______ stage involves two herculean tasks to write successfully.
______ is a system of communication that states a meticulously crafted process to come up with a productive output.
Taking a cursory look at your idea can be categorized into the ______ stage of writing.
______ is the term used to describe the way a text establishes links between sentences.
The topic sentence is usually written ______.
options: at the beginning of the sentence
at the middle of the sentence
at the end of the sentence
at the beginning of the sentence only

questions.question: One of these is NOT an importance of narrative writing.
options: It must have a moral or message
It must have a perspective/point of view
questions.answer_text: It must have a perspective/point of view

questions.question: ______ refers to the sentence that states or indicates the idea a paragraph is about.
______ has to do with a paragraph having a central idea and maintaining same as the subject of discussion.
______ is the term used to describe grammatical means by which sentences and paragraphs are linked and the relationship between them established.
______ discusses the devices to ensure cohesion within and across paragraphs.
______ requires a beginning, middle and an end.
Carrying out a survey on the topic to determine the right manner of approach falls under the ______ stage of writing.
______ is the main purpose of writing an essay.
options: The concluding part of an essay

questions.question: One of these is NOT a good way of concluding an essay.
You can gather information through outdated books.
Building an essay draft is a stage of writing.
To compose means to ______.
Writing is an essential skill.
______ is a variety of language used for a particular purpose or in a particular communicative situation.
The principle of describing which is objective and indicates an observation or evaluation (authorial impression containing strategies and details) is ______.
One of these is NOT an approach to expository essay.
When you're composing a narrative, one of these is NOT an important factor.
options: It must cultivate and sustain the interest of the reader through suspense
A good narrative should involve the reader
Events should not capture what is realistic
Identifying the aspect of a specific incident
questions.answer_text: Events should not capture what is realistic

questions.question: Narrative writing deals with stories, people, and events.
You are able to differentiate disciplines because of the register.
Repeated words are allowed in academic writing.
______ is an extremely detailed process and it is best when performed by a professional.
The term 'register' is used to refer to whether language is being used formally or informally.
Registers vary because the language is used for different purposes in different contexts and for different audiences.
Registers can be said to be related to disciplines and areas of specialization, for example a legal register, advertising register, banking register, weather forecasting register.
______ means writing every idea that comes to your head.
While editing, checking for repetition, clarity, grammar, spelling and punctuation is important.
One of these is NOT a process of writing.
While revising, one of these is NOT important.
options: Add to what you have if it seems insufficient
Rearrange the flow to make a better read
Remove the words that made the work redundant
Replace them with something else to get your readers non-enthusiastic about your writing
questions.answer_text: Replace them with something else to get your readers non-enthusiastic about your writing

questions.question: While editing, checking for repetition, clarity, grammar, spelling and punctuation is important.
We have ______ vowel sounds in English.
When we write essays, we reflect our thoughts, opinions and ideas.
______ involves looking at and understanding the meaning of letters and symbols.
Speaker A: 'Ade killed the goat.' Speaker B: 'The goat was killed by Ade.' This represents ______.
______ is the last of the language skills.
Ideas, observations, opinions, suggestions, criticisms are best produced through the ______ skill.
One of these is NOT a type of writing.
Which of these is NOT part of academic words?
One of these is NOT true about academic writing.
In what writing do we have recommendation?
Social/informal writing entertains.
Academic writing involves ______.
______ writing is mainly used in an academic setting.
One of these is NOT true of writing.
options: It makes use of cues only

questions.question: In ______ writing, there is jettisoning of colloquialism.
Which of these is NOT true about academic writing?
'Conclusively' and 'in conclusion' have the same meaning.
Precision in academic writing deals with facts.
Hedging your ideas is important mainly in academic/report writing.
Poems can be categorized as ______.
You're responsible for what you are writing about.
Casual presentation of thought is categorized under ______.
Academic writing only educates and instructs.
______ is a whole system and structure of a language usually consisting of syntax, morphology and sometimes phonology and semantics.
______ is a group of words that have the same basic behaviour.
______ comes before a word while ______ comes after a word.
The NAVA group (Noun, verb, adjective and adverb) are classified under ______, as they admit new words.
______ word group are static including pronoun, article. They don't admit new words.
'The children play football always. We went to watch a play at TMA theatre.' Play here serves as ______.
______ comprises lexical words which have dictionary meaning.
______ word class comprises functional words having no meaning on their own.
A form of writing with the main objective of informing rather than entertainment is known as ______.
______ is a set of customs and rules for polite behaviour especially among a particular class of people or in a particular profession.
______ serves as the only form of communication for most people in business and communication.
Social standards are to be adhered to in order to avoid miscommunications that might lead to negative notions about persons in email etiquette.
There are ______ types of writing styles.
______ refers to the expectation that the standard written form of English language is used in academic writings.
One of these is formal.
The first act in perfecting the art of academic writing is ______.
The name of some quality or non-material thing e.g. intelligence, charity, laziness is known as ______.
Writing is a useful lifelong skill.
______ shows belonging or possession i.e. mine, yours, his, her, etc.
There are 3 persons in grammar: first (person speaking), second (person spoken to), third (person/thing spoken about).
______ are verbs which form their past tense and past participle by following the standard rule (suffix '-d' or '-ed').
'Abimbola gave him a pen.' Pen is the ______.
'The man gave her a beautiful ring' is an example of ______ sentence.
'The boy came and he opened the door because no one answered him' is a ______ sentence.
'The students were diligent but they did not pass their examination' is an example of ______ sentence.
'I love the little girl because she is pleasant' is an example of ______ sentence.
______ sentence is used to convey information.
'She bought some apples' is a ______ sentence.
Sentences can be classified into functions and structures.
The past participle of 'boil' is ______.
The past form of 'boil' is ______.
Auxiliary verbs are 'BE, TO and HAVE'.
She ______ in Ado.
The boys ______ in Oye-Ekiti.
______ is the literal meaning of a word, that is, the dictionary definition of a word.
______ is the associative meaning one can connect to a word, or emotional suggestions related to that word.
According to the text, what is academic writing NOT solely about?
Which of the following is NOT listed as a common use for academic writing?
What do ethical standards in academic writing primarily guide?
options: How fast a student can write an essay
How to properly use informal language
How information is gathered, presented, and acknowledged
How to manipulate facts without getting caught
questions.answer_text: How information is gathered, presented, and acknowledged

questions.question: How is 'writing ethics' defined?
options: The speed of completing an academic project
The moral principles and standards that guide writers
The use of advanced vocabulary in research
The strict rules of standard English grammar
questions.answer_text: The moral principles and standards that guide writers

questions.question: Why is ethics important in academic writing?
options: Because it helps writers sell more books
Because academic works contribute to knowledge and learning
Because it prevents teachers from grading too strictly
Because it is required to pass informal writing classes
questions.answer_text: Because academic works contribute to knowledge and learning

questions.question: Which of these is considered a dishonest practice in academic writing?
Who should a good academic writer consider the impact of their writing on?
options: Readers, the academic community, and society
questions.answer_text: Readers, the academic community, and society

questions.question: What does the element of 'Honesty' involve in academic writing?
options: Presenting another person's ideas as your own
Being truthful about the work done and sources used
Writing without using any external sources
questions.answer_text: Being truthful about the work done and sources used

questions.question: How does using sources correctly affect an academic work?
options: It weakens the writer's original ideas
It shows the writer cannot think independently
It strengthens the work by showing ideas are supported by reliable information
It makes the work too long and boring to read
questions.answer_text: It strengthens the work by showing ideas are supported by reliable information

questions.question: What does 'Originality' refer to in academic writing?
options: Copying old facts directly into a new paper
Depending completely on other people's work
The ability to produce fresh, independent, and meaningful ideas
Avoiding the use of citations and references
questions.answer_text: The ability to produce fresh, independent, and meaningful ideas

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a way a work may show originality?
What is described as the direct opposite of plagiarism?
What does 'Transparency' mean in the context of writing ethics?
options: Hiding the methods used in research
Keeping your sources a secret from the reader

questions.question: What does transparent writing allow readers to understand?
options: The personal life and habits of the writer
How the writer gathered information and the process used
The grammatical rules of the English language
questions.answer_text: How the writer gathered information and the process used

questions.question: Which element of writing ethics means taking responsibility for one's writing?
An accountable writer is willing to do which of the following?
options: Blame others for inaccuracies in their work
Deny all errors pointed out by readers
Defend their arguments and correct mistakes when necessary
Hide the materials used for research
questions.answer_text: Defend their arguments and correct mistakes when necessary

questions.question: What does 'Fairness' mean in academic writing?
options: Presenting ideas in an emotional and aggressive manner
Presenting ideas and arguments in a balanced and unbiased manner
Making exaggerated claims to win an argument
questions.answer_text: Presenting ideas and arguments in a balanced and unbiased manner

questions.question: Which of the following is a writer expected to avoid in order to maintain fairness?
What is the ethical purpose of considering opposing opinions in academic writing?
options: To confuse the reader and make the text longer
To maintain fairness and present arguments objectively
To increase the word count of the essay quickly
To prove that the opposing opinions are completely worthless
questions.answer_text: To maintain fairness and present arguments objectively

questions.question: What does 'Respect for Sources' entail?
options: Recognizing that creative works belong to their creators
Modifying someone else's work to make it look like your own
Reading a lot of books before you begin writing
Only using sources that are free to the public
questions.answer_text: Recognizing that creative works belong to their creators

questions.question: What must be provided whenever information is taken from a source?
options: A personal thank-you letter to the author
Financial compensation to the original author
A new, original title for the source

questions.question: What happens if a writer fails to acknowledge sources properly?
options: The work becomes more original and creative
It saves the reader time and effort
It is unethical and may damage the writer's credibility
It makes the writing much more transparent
questions.answer_text: It is unethical and may damage the writer's credibility

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT one of the major elements of writing ethics discussed?
What is the result of following ethical principles in academic writing?
options: The writing becomes more meaningful, reliable, and valuable to society
The writer will never make a grammatical mistake
The writer is guaranteed to get the highest possible grade
The writer will not have to do any independent research
questions.answer_text: The writing becomes more meaningful, reliable, and valuable to society

questions.question: Which of these statements is true based on the text?
options: Academic writing is exactly the same as informal writing
Academic writing should rely entirely on other people's work to be accurate
Academic writing follows clear rules and standards
Responsible writers blame their sources for any inaccuracies found in their work
questions.answer_text: Academic writing follows clear rules and standards
